audienceSections.normal: In a shared plan for the market, you will use [[grammar:Vamos a + infinitivo]]. Use [[grammar:Vamos a]] plus an infinitive for a shared near-future plan. Vamos a comprar fruta means ‘We are going to buy fruit.’ Do not confuse it with vamos al mercado, where al marks a destination.
audienceSections.expert: This chapter presents [[grammar:Vamos a + infinitivo]] in a shared plan for the market. First-person plural vamos plus a and infinitive forms the periphrastic future. Destination al is the contraction of a + el and does not introduce an infinitive.

audienceSections.normal: Use [[grammar:Vamos a]] plus an infinitive for a shared near-future plan. Vamos a comprar fruta means ‘We are going to buy fruit.’ Do not confuse it with vamos al mercado, where al marks a destination.
audienceSections.expert: First-person plural vamos plus a and infinitive forms the periphrastic future. Destination al is the contraction of a + el and does not introduce an infinitive.

audienceSections.normal: Read the Spanish punctuation with the sentence: ¿ starts a question and ¡ starts an exclamation. Learn nouns with el or la when an article is shown.
audienceSections.expert: The reading uses broadly intelligible contemporary Spanish with tú as neutral informal singular address. Null subjects and article-bearing citation forms are analyzed only where attested.

breakdown.normal:
Read each exact spanish line, then check its natural English meaning. Watch for [[grammar:Vamos a + infinitivo]] where it appears.

- ¿Vamos al mercado hoy? — Shall we go to the market today?
- Sí, vamos a comprar fruta. — Yes, we are going to buy fruit.
- También quiero tomates. — I also want tomatoes.
- El mercado está cerca de la casa. — The market is near the house.
- ¿Vamos juntos a las tres? — Shall we go together at three?
- Sí, nos vemos allí. — Yes, see you there.
breakdown.expert:
These exact reading lines provide the distributional evidence for [[grammar:Vamos a + infinitivo]]. The grammar section above analyzes the relevant morphology and syntax.

- ¿Vamos al mercado hoy? — Shall we go to the market today?
- Sí, vamos a comprar fruta. — Yes, we are going to buy fruit.
- También quiero tomates. — I also want tomatoes.
- El mercado está cerca de la casa. — The market is near the house.
- ¿Vamos juntos a las tres? — Shall we go together at three?
- Sí, nos vemos allí. — Yes, see you there.
